div1.entryFree:
Bearer, 1) one who carries a burden: my dull b. (i. e. the horse) Sonn. 51, 2.
2) one who conveys and delivers a letter or a message: LLL IV, 1, 55. Hml. I, 2, 35 (Ff. for bearing). V, 2, 46.
3) sufferer: when crouching marrow in the b. strong cries of itself No more, Tim. V, 4, 9.
4) wearer, owner: O majesty, when thou dost pinch thy b., thou dost sit like a rich armour, H4B IV, 5, 29. thou (the crown) hast eat thy b. up, H4B IV, 5, 29 though it (pomp) be temporal, yet if fortune divorce it from the b. H8 II, 3, 15. the beauty that is borne here in the face the b. knows not, Troil. III, 3, 104.
